threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep looking for parking spots like you’re at a shopping mall. It’s a lost cause here, mate.
Last Saturday, the 333 was almost empty except for a guy loudly arguing with his own reflection in the window. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the taxi driver get out to help a lost tourist figure out the BVG app, I knew I was home. Community doesn't just mean neighbors.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a currywurst stand open at 3 AM, crowded with people discussing philosophy while waiting for their sausage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to avoid BART delays and opt for the 33 instead, even if it means standing in line for a torta first.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 in traffic with a saxophonist playing a solo. The driver rolled the window down, and the whole bus just vibed to the street performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists miss how quiet Gràcia can get on a Sunday; locals are hiding away, enjoying the stillness while they recharge for the week ahead.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They're the ones marveling at the 1€ menú del dia while texting in English, oblivious to the local chatter around them.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Stokey' instead of the proper 'Stoke Newington'—the locals will definitely notice and smirk.
Caught a late 59 the other night; a guy was singing to himself so loudly, even the driver had to smile. Brixton after dark is something else.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a guy rapping in a mix of German and Turkish on the corner while a stray cat lounged on a fancy café's terrace. Berlin magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you have spontaneous techno parties in random warehouses, where the smoke is thick and the music doesn't stop for 48 hours.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the tacos at La Taqueria taste even better after a dive at the Elbo Room. Tourists just hit the spots on Yelp.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy playing a violin on the 31-Balboa—one hand gripping his case, the other bowing like he’s performing at the SF Symphony. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but locals know the best tapas spots are hidden in the alleys, away from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they struggle with the metro ticket machines – staring intensely like it's a Rubik's cube.
